title: Microsoft to build a 2 GW data center in Texas with Chevron natural gas
summary: The data center will have its own gas plant to avoid overloading the power grid, in a 20-year agreement.

title: Google invests $75 million in A24 to develop AI research
summary: Google DeepMind and the film studio will collaborate on creating AI tools for film production.

title: Getty Images signs multimillion-dollar deal with OpenAI to license its photos for ChatGPT
summary: Getty Images' stock price surges after the announcement of the agreement to integrate its catalog into the AI search engine.

title: Sakana AI launches Fugu, a system that combines multiple language models to compete with Anthropic
summary: Fugu orchestrates several LLMs and achieves results comparable to Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models in benchmark tests.

title: UK to ban social media for under-16s in 2027
summary: The founder of 'Safe TikTok' claims that the United States will follow the UK's lead in restricting access for minors.

title: AI-generated content dominates TikTok and YouTube, according to their own executives
summary: More than half of videos on TikTok are created with AI, while YouTube seeks to preserve human authenticity.

title: Windows 11 suffers multiple bugs: hibernation damages SSD and update blocks apps
summary: The latest Windows 11 update makes Office and OneDrive inaccessible, while hibernation mode shortens disk life.

title: Meta appoints Kunal Shah as new head of WhatsApp and admits staff demotivation
summary: CRED founder takes over the messaging app, while a senior executive acknowledges that employee morale is at rock bottom.

title: AI poses a cybersecurity threat according to Five Eyes alliance and the Anthropic case
summary: Intelligence services warn that AI models can transform cyberattacks, while Anthropic's Mythos model allegedly breached classified NSA systems.

title: Privacy in question: facial recognition and use of police apps spark controversy
summary: An article warns of the risks of giving up personal images, while officers are arrested for using Flock tool to harass citizens.

title: The World Cup boosts terrace business in Asturias and also scams
summary: The sporting event increases activity in bars but also gives rise to new scams that take advantage of World Cup expectations.

title: Google bets on its TPU chips to compete with NVIDIA while NVIDIA prepares its Blackwell-Next GPUs
summary: Google invests billions in its AI processors to challenge NVIDIA's dominance, which is planning new graphics architectures.

title: Gaming industry loses Ubisoft co-founder and Epic Games executive
summary: Claude Guillemot dies in a plane crash and Sjoerd De Jong leaves Epic Games, marking a critical moment for the sector.

title: Data breach at Klue and new OXLOADER malware campaign threaten cybersecurity
summary: The breach on the competitive intelligence platform affects several security companies, while a loader distributes the CastleStealer malware.

title: Bipartisan deal in US for children's online safety and judicial setback in Ohio
summary: The internet safety bill for minors advances in Congress while a court blocks social media restrictions in Ohio.

title: DHL adds wind-powered ships and a robotic arm to charge electric vehicles
summary: The logistics company commits to sustainable transport with wind ships, while a robot automates EV charging.

title: Google tightens developer verification on Android and Android 17 arrives with bugs on Pixel
summary: Developers from four countries will have to verify their identity on Google Play, while Android 17 has touch and freezing issues.

title: postmarketOS 26.06 and Mesa 26.2 update free software with major new features
summary: The mobile distribution includes GNOME 50 and KDE Plasma 6.6, while NVIDIA NVK drivers add DLSS support.

title: Financial market moves with Greenspan and gains transparency with British bonds
summary: Alan Greenspan's famous phrase about markets and real-time access to UK debt data mark current financial news.

title: Amazon invests $50 billion in OpenAI and Samsung deploys ChatGPT Enterprise in Korea
summary: The e-commerce giant bets on OpenAI's artificial intelligence, while Samsung integrates its tools into South Korean offices.
